2020-06-24.abstract: As mass infections strike even in places that had seemed to tame the coronavirus, officials are turning to targeted and fast-but-flexible approaches to stop third or fourth waves.

2020-06-24.title: U.S. Sets Record for Daily New Cases as Virus Surges in South and West
2020-06-24.abstract: Public health officials in the United States reported 36,880 new cases on Wednesday. Houston’s intensive-care units are running out of available beds, the mayor said.

2020-06-24.title: Hit Hard by Coronavirus, Russia Holds a Mostly Mask-Free Victory Parade
2020-06-24.abstract: Tens of thousands turned out for the delayed annual celebration of Russia’s defeat of Nazi Germany but few, including veterans in their 80s and 90s, took precautions.

2020-06-24.title: For North Korea, Blowing Hot and Cold Is Part of the Strategy
2020-06-24.abstract: Alternating between raising tensions and ​​extending an olive branch​ — all to confuse the enemy — has been part of the regime’s dog-eared playbook. ​

2020-06-25.title: For Those Getting Married, a Searchable Domestic Violence Database
2020-06-25.abstract: A Chinese city is rolling out the database to allow people to check if their partners have a history of abuse. Domestic violence has been exacerbated by lockdowns during the coronavirus pandemic.

2020-06-25.title: As New Coronavirus Cases Hit Another Record in the U.S., Some States Delay Reopenings
2020-06-25.abstract: The U.S. reported more than 41,000 new cases on Thursday, the second consecutive day with a record total, and the C.D.C. said the true national caseload was probably 10 times the official count. India plans to test all 29 million residents of its capital for the virus.

2020-06-25.title: Congo’s Deadliest Ebola Outbreak Is Declared Over
2020-06-25.abstract: The World Health Organization called the end of the country’s 10th outbreak, the second deadliest in history, “a victory for science.” Health workers had faced mistrust and treatment centers were attacked.

2020-06-25.title: 22 Police Officers Injured in Scuffles at Illegal London Block Party
2020-06-25.abstract: Officers were attacked as they attempted to break up an outdoor crowd in the southern district of Brixton, the police said.

2020-06-25.title: Israel Announces Partnership With U.A.E., Which Throws Cold Water On It
2020-06-25.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu touted a rare public opening between the two countries. Apparently, the Emirates was not ready for it.

2020-06-26.title: Li Zhensheng, Photographer of China’s Cultural Revolution, Dies at 79
2020-06-26.abstract: With his camera and red arm band, he captured the dark side of Mao’s revolution at great personal risk. Many of the images were stashed under a floor, going undeveloped for years.

2020-06-26.title: Australian Politician’s Home Raided in Chinese Influence Inquiry
2020-06-26.abstract: The case is the first high-profile criminal investigation of Chinese influence peddling to be made public since Australia passed foreign interference laws two years ago.

2020-06-26.title: Seven Colombian Soldiers Charged in Rape of Indigenous Girl
2020-06-26.abstract: The charges, announced by the attorney general, follow years of accusations of illegal killings. The defendants pleaded guilty in a closed hearing.

2020-06-26.title: China’s Military Provokes Its Neighbors, but the Message Is for the United States
2020-06-26.abstract: From the Himalayas to the South China Sea, China is pressing its territorial claims aggressively, raising the possibility of additional deadly clashes.

2020-06-26.title: Horses and Reporting in the Field
2020-06-26.abstract: After weeks of lockdown, getting in my car to cover a story was both a surreal and welcome experience.

2020-06-27.title: In Seizing Control, China Sidelines Its Allies in Hong Kong
2020-06-27.abstract: Local tycoons and British-trained civil servants helped promote Beijing’s agenda in the territory. Now Beijing seems ready to push them aside.

2020-06-27.title: How the World Missed Covid-19’s Silent Spread
2020-06-27.abstract: Symptomless transmission makes the coronavirus far harder to fight. But health officials dismissed the risk for months, pushing misleading and contradictory claims in the face of mounting evidence.

2020-06-27.title: U.S. States Backtrack on Reopening as Coronavirus Cases Climb
2020-06-27.abstract: Mississippi and Houston are among the areas in danger of being overwhelmed. The resurgent virus has left many Americans questioning whether earlier lockdowns were worth their sacrifices.

2020-06-27.title: Micheal Martin Becomes Ireland’s Prime Minister as Rival Parties Ally
2020-06-27.abstract: The country’s new coalition government, formed across a historic political fault line after four months of negotiations, faces headwinds from Brexit, the pandemic and a housing crisis.

2020-06-27.title: Afghan Deaths Pile Up in Uncertainty Over U.S. Deal With Taliban
2020-06-27.abstract: Targeted killings and widespread attacks across Afghanistan have sapped brief optimism as peace talks remain stalled.

2020-06-28.title: What China’s New National Security Law Means for Hong Kong
2020-06-28.abstract: Chinese lawmakers have approved the law, threatening to curb free speech and protest in Hong Kong after a year of political tumult.

2020-06-28.title: African Migrants in Yemen Scapegoated for Coronavirus Outbreak
2020-06-28.abstract: Migrants say Houthi militias that control northern Yemen are brutally forcing them out of their territory and into dangerous situations.

2020-06-28.title: The Global Death Toll Now Tops 500,000
2020-06-28.abstract: Vice President Mike Pence asserts that increased testing explains a surge in cases, but experts and evidence say otherwise. Houston’s top official has self-quarantined.

2020-06-28.title: Voters in Poland Appear to Force a Runoff for President
2020-06-28.abstract: The incumbent, Andrzej Duda, will face Warsaw’s mayor, Rafal Trzaskowski, on July 12, according to exit polls. Turnout was high in Europe’s first major election during the pandemic.

2020-06-28.title: Taiwan Celebrates Pride With a Public Parade
2020-06-28.abstract: Hundreds joined a march in Taipei, making it one of the few places in the world to proceed with such an event during the coronavirus pandemic.

2020-06-29.title: Gunmen Wage Deadly Battle at Pakistan Stock Exchange
2020-06-29.abstract: Police and paramilitary forces surrounded the exchange in Karachi during a firefight in which at least three security officers and four attackers were killed.

2020-06-29.title: Coronavirus Is Battering Africa’s Growing Middle Class
2020-06-29.abstract: From Kenya to Nigeria, South Africa to Rwanda, the pandemic is decimating the livelihoods of the once-stable workers who were helping to drive Africa’s economic expansion.

2020-06-29.title: States Pause Plans to Reopen as Cases Soar
2020-06-29.abstract: 54,000 deaths in the U.S. were linked to nursing homes. A top C.D.C. official warned, “We are not even beginning to be over this.” At least 95 people who visited a Michigan bar have tested positive.

2020-06-29.title: Another Young Leader Taken. Afghans Ask: How Many More?
2020-06-29.abstract: Fatima ‘Natasha’ Khalil, 24, was a human rights worker shaped by two decades of struggle. She joins a painfully long list of young Afghans who died trying to help their country.

2020-06-29.title: At Least 32 Dead as Boat Capsizes in Bangladesh
2020-06-29.abstract: Search efforts were continuing after a passenger ship was struck by a ferry near a busy port in Dhaka, the capital.

2020-06-30.title: New Security Law Gives China Sweeping Powers Over Hong Kong
2020-06-30.abstract: The law, approved in Beijing with speed and secrecy and signed off by Xi Jinping, will tighten the Communist Party’s grip on Hong Kong after last year’s protests.

2020-06-30.title: NASA Scientist, Detained in Turkey for Years, Returns to U.S.
2020-06-30.abstract: Serkan Golge, an American caught up in increasingly fraught Turkish-U.S. relations, arrived with his family in Washington after nearly four years of imprisonment and house arrest.

2020-06-30.title: Australia Spending Nearly $1 Billion on Cyberdefense as China Tensions Rise
2020-06-30.abstract: Officials promised to recruit at least 500 cyberspies and build on the country’s offensive capabilities to take the online battle overseas.

2020-06-30.title: U.S. Cases Reach New Record
2020-06-30.abstract: More than 48,000 coronavirus cases were announced across the country and eight states announced single-day highs. Europe opens its doors — just not to Americans.

2020-06-30.title: Scientists Say New Strain of Swine Flu Virus Is Spreading to Humans in China
2020-06-30.abstract: A new study warns that the strain of H1N1, common on China’s pig farms since 2016, should be “urgently” controlled to avoid another pandemic.
